2606.22216.title: Delta-Diffusion: Modeling Longitudinal Brain Amyloid-PET Trajectories via Conditional Poisson Diffusion Bridge
2606.22216.paper_summary: While longitudinal brain PET imaging is the gold standard for quantifying the spatiotemporal accumulation of Beta-amyloid, its widespread clinical utility is constrained by high operational costs and cumulative radiation risks. Recent deep generative models show promise in longitudinal image synthesis; however, they often fail to capture subtle pathological progression due to identity drift and a persistent bias toward trivially replicating baseline signal intensities rather than modeling temporal transition. To this end, we propose Delta-Diffusion, a novel progression-aware framework that redefines longitudinal PET synthesis as a conditional Poisson Diffusion Bridge (PDB) process. Unlike standard diffusion models that start from Gaussian noise, our PDB formulation is mathematically anchored to the subject's baseline PET, effectively transforming the generative task into a conditional distribution transition of the amyloid trajectory. To handle heteroscedastic nature of PET imaging, we introduce a physically-grounded Poisson perturbation within a Diffusion Transformer (DiT). This architecture uses adaptive scale-shift modulation to precisely calibrate the synthesis with the elapsed clinical interval and structural MRI context. A volume-of-interest balanced objective is designed to emphasize sparse, high-risk regions of amyloid accumulation. Validated on two cohorts with 542 subjects, Delta-Diffusion demonstrates superior performance in capturing longitudinal variations in amyloid deposition compared to state-of-the-art methods, offering a robust computational framework for tracking disease progression.
2606.22216.paper_summary_zh: 雖然縱向腦部PET成像是量化Beta-淀粉樣蛋白時空累積的金標準，但其廣泛的臨床應用受到高運營成本和累積輻射風險的限制。最近的深度生成模型在縱向影像合成方面顯示出潛力；然而，它們往往無法捕捉微妙的病理進展，這是由於身份漂移和持續偏向於平凡地複製基線信號強度而不是建模時間過渡。為此，我們提出了Delta-Diffusion，一種新穎的進展感知框架，將縱向PET合成重新定義為條件泊松擴散橋（PDB）過程。與從高斯噪聲開始的標準擴散模型不同，我們的PDB公式在數學上依賴於受試者的基線PET，有效地將生成任務轉化為淀粉樣蛋白軌跡的條件分佈轉換。為了處理PET成像的異方差性質，我們在擴散Transformer（DiT）中引入了物理基礎的泊松擾動。這種架構使用自適應尺度偏移調製，精確地將合成與經過的臨床時間間隔和結構MRI上下文進行校準。設計了一個關注感興趣區域的平衡目標，以強調淀粉樣蛋白累積的稀疏高風險區域。在542名受試者的兩個隊列上進行驗證，Delta-Diffusion在捕捉淀粉樣沉積的縱向變化方面顯示出優於最先進方法的性能，提供了一個穩健的計算框架來追蹤疾病進展。
2606.22216.author: Yongheng Sun et.al.
2606.22216.authors: Yongheng Sun, Minhui Yu, Mengqi Wu, Maureen Kohi, Mingxia Liu
